item.title: A phase microscope for quantum gases
item.author: J. C. Brüggenjürgen
item.content:encoded:
Coherence properties are central to quantum systems and are at the heart of phenomena such as superconductivity. In this work, we studied coherence properties of an ultracold Bose gas in a two-dimensional optical lattice across the thermal phase ...

J. C. Brüggenjürgen

Science

item.title: Relativistic collapse of the classical triple bond in the CBi− molecular ion
item.author: Deniz Kahraman
item.content:encoded:
The conventional framework for chemical bonding between main-group elements involves separate σ and π orbitals to describe multiple bonds. However, relativistic effects mix these orbitals in molecules containing heavy elements through spin–orbit coupling,...

Deniz Kahraman

item.author: Ellen Wohl

item.title: Observation of disorder-free localization using a (2+1)D lattice gauge theory on a quantum processor
item.author: Google Quantum AI and Collaborators†
item.content:encoded:
Disorder-induced phenomena in quantum many-body systems pose a challenge for analytical and numerical approaches at relevant time and system scales. To reduce the cost of disorder sampling, we investigated quantum circuits initialized in states that form ...

Google Quantum AI and Collaborators†

Science

item.title: Orbital magnetoresistance in the antiferromagnet CoO driven by dynamic orbital angular momentum
item.author: Christin Schmitt
item.content:encoded:
Recent predictions of orders of magnitude larger orbital current effects compared with spin currents have attracted considerable interest. However, orbital currents must first be converted into spin currents to interact with the static magnetization ...

Christin Schmitt

item.title: Illuminating the molecular basis of human daylight vision
item.author: Sarah L. Schmidt
item.content:encoded:
Photopic vision, including fast motion and color perception in daylight, is mediated by cone opsins, specialized G protein–coupled receptors (GPCRs). Despite sharing the same chromophore, the three receptor subtypes absorb light at different wavelengths ...

Sarah L. Schmidt

Science

item.title: Structural insights into spectral tuning and retinal exchange in cone visual pigments
item.author: Sayaka Ohashi
item.content:encoded:
Color vision in catarrhine primates relies on red-, green-, and blue-sensitive cone pigments that share an 11-cis-retinal chromophore but differ in absorption maxima. Red and green pigments arose by recent gene duplication and differ at only a few ...

Sayaka Ohashi

Science

item.title: Cryo–electron microscopy structures of human cone visual pigments
item.author: Qi Peng
item.content:encoded:
Human trichromatic color vision relies on three cone opsins [long-, middle-, and short-wavelength-sensitive opsins (LWS-, MWS-, and SWS-opsins, respectively)], whereas scotopic rod vision is mediated by rhodopsin. Although the structure of rhodopsin was ...

Qi Peng

Science

item.title: Iron-catalyzed active lipid peroxides drive ultrafast collective cell death in blooming algae
item.author: Yinjie Zhu
item.content:encoded:
Harmful algal blooms, the most severe ecological hazards worldwide, terminate abruptly within a few days. In this work, we identified that iron-catalyzed active lipid peroxides predominantly trigger individual cell ferroptosis and drive the population ...

Yinjie Zhu

Science

item.title: Structural N- and O-glycans revealed by high-resolution cryo-EM analysis of tubular mastigonemes
item.author: Junhao Huang黄隽豪
item.content:encoded:
The chemical complexity and nontemplated biosynthesis of glycans have posed considerable challenges for establishing sequence-structure relationships. Here we report cryo–electron microscopy structures of tubular mastigonemes from a golden alga species, ...

Junhao Huang黄隽豪

Science

item.title: Nano–electron volt Fourier-limited transition of a single surface-adsorbed molecule
item.author: Masoud Mirzaei
item.content:encoded:
High-resolution spectroscopy allows the probing of weak interactions and subtle phenomena. Although such measurements are routinely performed in the gas phase and in crystalline materials, studies of adsorbed species on surfaces have previously fallen ...

Masoud Mirzaei

Science

item.title: Has NSF slashed research to support tech initiative?
item.author: Jeffrey Mervis

Jeffrey Mervis

Science

item.title: Dynamic asymmetric strain imprinted into substrates by an oxide thin film
item.author: Elliot Kisiel
item.content:encoded:
In film-substrate systems, the substrate role is often considered to be limited to providing static mechanical constraints. Dynamic film-substrate interactions when a structural change in the film modifies the substrate are generally disregarded. Using ...

Elliot Kisiel

item.title: Indium-free perovskite/silicon tandem solar cells with tin oxide recombination layer and electrodes
item.author: Wei Shi施伟
item.content:encoded:
Indium-based transparent conductive oxides are widely used as electrodes and recombination layers in perovskite/silicon tandem solar cells, yet their scalability is constrained by indium scarcity and sputtering-induced damage. We report high-efficiency ...

Wei Shi施伟

Science
